page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical Intel Xeon 6455B cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes 60 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities, with the Geekbench single-core score reaching 2192.0, placing it in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression at 34.63 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.54 GB/sec also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%. However, multi-core and large-block memory operations show relative weaknesses. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 4318.97 and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression of 27.67 GB/sec fall into the bottom 25% tier. Uncached large-block read memory bandwidth is also limited, registering at 36.01 GB/sec.

The high clock speed of the Intel Xeon processor combined with DDR5 memory provides a high-frequency compute environment for memory-intensive tasks, though the low physical core count limits parallel processing efficiency. This resource density makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high single-thread throughput and substantial memory per core rather than massive parallelization. Supported instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX assist in specialized vector and matrix operations. The instance is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, memory-bound applications, and lightweight database workloads that do not require high multi-core scaling or local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from 2 physical Intel Xeon 6455B cores running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmarks highlight exceptional single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core scores in the top 10% and strong single-threaded compression results. Conversely, multi-core execution and uncached large-block memory reads show weaker performance, placing in the bottom 25%. The hardware profile, which includes AVX-512 and AMX extensions, is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads requiring high single-thread performance rather than dense parallelization. It is suited for memory-bound applications, single-threaded processing, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier single-core performance but weaker multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and high single-core Intel Xeon performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized configuration delivering exceptional single-core clock speeds and DDR5 memory for high-frequency, memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6455B processor with a high 3.9 GHz base clock speed
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and compression benchmarks
Weaker multi-core scaling and uncached large-block memory read speeds
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
Suited for memory-bound applications and single-threaded processing tasks
